framework.name: PCI DSS
framework.description: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard v4.0 — security standards for organizations that handle branded credit cards

controls.domain: Install and Maintain Network Security Controls
controls.title: Security Policies and Procedures Defined and Known
controls.requirementText: All security policies and operational procedures that are identified in Requirement 1 are documented, kept up to date, in use, and known to all affected parties.
controls.guidance: Maintain documented policies and procedures for network security controls and ensure they are communicated to all relevant personnel.

controls.domain: Install and Maintain Network Security Controls
controls.title: Roles and Responsibilities Assigned
controls.requirementText: Roles and responsibilities for performing activities in Requirement 1 are documented, assigned, and understood.
controls.guidance: Document and assign roles and responsibilities for network security control management to specific personnel.

controls.domain: Install and Maintain Network Security Controls
controls.title: NSC Configuration Standards Defined and Implemented
controls.requirementText: Configuration standards for NSC rulesets are defined, implemented, and maintained.
controls.guidance: Define and implement configuration standards for all network security controls including firewalls, routers, and cloud security groups.

controls.domain: Install and Maintain Network Security Controls
controls.title: NSC Configuration Changes Reviewed
controls.requirementText: All changes to network connections and to configurations of NSCs are approved and managed in accordance with the change control process defined at Requirement 6.5.1.
controls.guidance: Implement change management processes for all network security control configuration changes with proper approval workflows.

controls.domain: Install and Maintain Network Security Controls
controls.title: Permitted Services, Protocols, and Ports Identified
controls.requirementText: All services, protocols, and ports that are allowed are identified, approved, and have a defined business need.
controls.guidance: Maintain documentation of all permitted services, protocols, and ports with business justification for each.

controls.domain: Install and Maintain Network Security Controls
controls.title: Security Features for Insecure Services Documented
controls.requirementText: Security features are defined and implemented for all services, protocols, and ports that are in use and considered to be insecure, such that the risk is mitigated.
controls.guidance: For any insecure services or protocols in use, document compensating security controls that mitigate the associated risks.

controls.domain: Install and Maintain Network Security Controls
controls.title: NSC Configurations Reviewed Semi-Annually
controls.requirementText: Configurations of NSCs are reviewed at least once every six months to confirm they are relevant and effective.
controls.guidance: Conduct semi-annual reviews of all network security control configurations to ensure they remain appropriate and effective.

controls.domain: Install and Maintain Network Security Controls
controls.title: Inbound Traffic to CDE Restricted
controls.requirementText: Inbound traffic to the CDE is restricted to only that traffic which is necessary, and all other traffic is specifically denied.
controls.guidance: Configure network security controls to restrict inbound traffic to the cardholder data environment to only necessary traffic with explicit deny-all rules.
evidenceRequirements: Firewall rule sets showing deny-all default

controls.domain: Install and Maintain Network Security Controls
controls.title: Outbound Traffic from CDE Restricted
controls.requirementText: Outbound traffic from the CDE is restricted to only that traffic which is necessary, and all other traffic is specifically denied.
controls.guidance: Configure network security controls to restrict outbound traffic from the CDE to only necessary traffic with explicit deny-all rules.
evidenceRequirements: Firewall rule sets for outbound traffic

controls.domain: Install and Maintain Network Security Controls
controls.subdomain: Network Connections Between Trusted and Untrusted Networks
controls.title: NSCs Between Trusted and Untrusted Networks
controls.requirementText: NSCs are implemented between trusted and untrusted networks.
controls.guidance: Deploy network security controls at all boundaries between trusted and untrusted network segments to control traffic flow.

controls.domain: Install and Maintain Network Security Controls
controls.subdomain: Network Connections Between Trusted and Untrusted Networks
controls.title: Inbound Traffic from Untrusted Networks Restricted
controls.requirementText: Inbound traffic from untrusted networks to trusted networks is restricted to communications with system components that are authorized to provide publicly accessible services, protocols, and ports.
controls.guidance: Restrict inbound traffic from untrusted networks to only authorized public-facing services with documented business justification.

controls.domain: Apply Secure Configurations to All System Components
controls.title: Security Policies and Procedures Defined
controls.requirementText: All security policies and operational procedures that are identified in Requirement 2 are documented, kept up to date, in use, and known to all affected parties.
controls.guidance: Maintain documented policies and procedures for secure configuration of all system components.

controls.domain: Apply Secure Configurations to All System Components
controls.title: Configuration Standards Developed
controls.requirementText: Configuration standards are developed, implemented, and maintained to cover all system components, address all known security vulnerabilities, be consistent with industry-accepted system hardening standards, and be updated as new vulnerability issues are identified.
controls.guidance: Develop configuration standards based on industry-accepted hardening guidelines (CIS Benchmarks, DISA STIGs) for all system components.

controls.domain: Apply Secure Configurations to All System Components
controls.title: Vendor Default Accounts Managed
controls.requirementText: Vendor default accounts are managed as follows: if the vendor default account(s) will be used, the default password is changed. If the vendor default account(s) will not be used, the account is removed or disabled.
controls.guidance: Change all vendor-supplied default passwords before deploying systems and disable or remove unused default accounts.

controls.domain: Apply Secure Configurations to All System Components
controls.title: Unnecessary Services Removed or Disabled
controls.requirementText: All unnecessary functionality is removed or disabled, such as unnecessary scripts, drivers, features, subsystems, file systems, unnecessary web servers.
controls.guidance: Remove or disable all unnecessary services, protocols, daemons, and functionality from system components to reduce the attack surface.

controls.domain: Protect Stored Account Data
controls.title: Security Policies and Procedures Defined
controls.requirementText: All security policies and operational procedures that are identified in Requirement 3 are documented, kept up to date, in use, and known to all affected parties.
controls.guidance: Maintain documented policies and procedures for protecting stored account data.

controls.domain: Protect Stored Account Data
controls.title: Data Retention and Disposal
controls.requirementText: Account data storage amount and retention time are limited to that which is required for business, legal, and/or regulatory purposes, as documented in the data retention policy.
controls.guidance: Implement data retention policies that minimize the amount and duration of stored account data to only what is required.

controls.domain: Protect Stored Account Data
controls.subdomain: Sensitive Authentication Data Not Stored After Authorization
controls.title: SAD Not Retained After Authorization
controls.requirementText: Sensitive authentication data (SAD) is not retained after authorization, even if encrypted. All sensitive authentication data received is rendered unrecoverable upon completion of the authorization process.
controls.guidance: Ensure sensitive authentication data (full track data, CAV2/CVC2/CVV2/CID, PINs) is not stored after authorization.
evidenceRequirements: Data flow analysis showing SAD handling

controls.domain: Protect Stored Account Data
controls.subdomain: Access to Stored PAN is Restricted
controls.title: PAN Masked When Displayed
controls.requirementText: PAN is masked when displayed (the first six and last four digits are the maximum number of digits to be displayed), such that only personnel with a legitimate business need can see more than the first six/last four digits of the PAN.
controls.guidance: Implement PAN masking to display only the first six and last four digits unless there is a documented business need for full PAN visibility.
evidenceRequirements: Business justification for full PAN access

controls.domain: Protect Stored Account Data
controls.title: PAN Rendered Unreadable Anywhere it is Stored
controls.requirementText: PAN is rendered unreadable anywhere it is stored by using any of the following approaches: one-way hashes based on strong cryptography, truncation, index tokens and pads, or strong cryptography with associated key-management processes and procedures.
controls.guidance: Render PAN unreadable wherever it is stored using strong cryptography, tokenization, truncation, or one-way hashing.

controls.domain: Protect Cardholder Data with Strong Cryptography During Transmission
controls.title: Security Policies and Procedures Defined
controls.requirementText: All security policies and operational procedures that are identified in Requirement 4 are documented, kept up to date, in use, and known to all affected parties.
controls.guidance: Maintain documented policies and procedures for protecting cardholder data during transmission over open, public networks.

controls.domain: Protect Cardholder Data with Strong Cryptography During Transmission
controls.title: Strong Cryptography Used During Transmission
controls.requirementText: PAN is protected with strong cryptography whenever it is sent via end-user messaging technologies. Only trusted keys and certificates are accepted. Certificates used to safeguard PAN during transmission over open, public networks are confirmed as valid and are not expired or revoked.
controls.guidance: Use strong cryptographic protocols (TLS 1.2 or higher) to protect PAN during transmission over open, public networks.

controls.domain: Protect All Systems and Networks from Malicious Software
controls.title: Security Policies and Procedures Defined
controls.requirementText: All security policies and operational procedures that are identified in Requirement 5 are documented, kept up to date, in use, and known to all affected parties.
controls.guidance: Maintain documented policies and procedures for protecting systems from malicious software.

controls.domain: Protect All Systems and Networks from Malicious Software
controls.subdomain: Malicious Software is Prevented or Detected
controls.title: Anti-Malware Solution Deployed
controls.requirementText: An anti-malware solution(s) is deployed on all system components, except for those system components identified in periodic evaluations that concludes the system components are not at risk from malware.
controls.guidance: Deploy anti-malware solutions on all system components that are susceptible to malware, with documented exceptions for systems deemed not at risk.

controls.domain: Protect All Systems and Networks from Malicious Software
controls.subdomain: Malicious Software is Prevented or Detected
controls.title: Anti-Malware Solution Detects All Known Types
controls.requirementText: The deployed anti-malware solution(s) detects all known types of malware and removes, blocks, or contains all known types of malware.
controls.guidance: Ensure anti-malware solutions are capable of detecting, blocking, and remediating all known malware types including viruses, trojans, worms, and ransomware.

controls.domain: Protect All Systems and Networks from Malicious Software
controls.subdomain: Malicious Software is Prevented or Detected
controls.title: System Components Evaluated for Malware Risk
controls.requirementText: Any system components that are not at risk for malware are evaluated periodically to include a documented list of all system components not at risk, identification and evaluation of evolving malware threats, and confirmation whether such system components continue to not require anti-malware protection.
controls.guidance: Periodically evaluate system components without anti-malware protection to confirm they remain low risk, documenting the evaluation and rationale.

controls.domain: Protect All Systems and Networks from Malicious Software
controls.subdomain: Anti-Malware Mechanisms are Active and Maintained
controls.title: Anti-Malware Solution Kept Current
controls.requirementText: The anti-malware solution(s) is kept current via automatic updates.
controls.guidance: Configure anti-malware solutions to receive automatic signature and engine updates to maintain effectiveness against emerging threats.

controls.domain: Protect All Systems and Networks from Malicious Software
controls.subdomain: Anti-Malware Mechanisms are Active and Maintained
controls.title: Anti-Malware Performs Periodic and Active Scans
controls.requirementText: The anti-malware solution(s) performs periodic scans and active or real-time scans, or performs continuous behavioral analysis of systems or processes.
controls.guidance: Configure anti-malware to perform both scheduled scans and real-time/on-access scanning or behavioral analysis.

controls.domain: Develop and Maintain Secure Systems and Software
controls.title: Security Policies and Procedures Defined
controls.requirementText: All security policies and operational procedures that are identified in Requirement 6 are documented, kept up to date, in use, and known to all affected parties.
controls.guidance: Maintain documented policies and procedures for developing and maintaining secure systems and software.

controls.domain: Develop and Maintain Secure Systems and Software
controls.subdomain: Bespoke and Custom Software Developed Securely
controls.title: Secure Development Training
controls.requirementText: Bespoke and custom software are developed securely, as follows: based on industry standards and/or best practices for secure development; in accordance with PCI DSS; and incorporating information security throughout the software-development life cycle.
controls.guidance: Ensure bespoke and custom software development follows industry-standard secure coding practices integrated throughout the SDLC.

controls.domain: Develop and Maintain Secure Systems and Software
controls.subdomain: Bespoke and Custom Software Developed Securely
controls.title: Software Engineering Techniques Prevent Attacks
controls.requirementText: Software engineering techniques or other methods are defined and in use by software development personnel to prevent or mitigate common software attacks and related vulnerabilities in bespoke and custom software, including but not limited to injection attacks, attacks on data and data structures, attacks on cryptography usage, attacks on business logic, attacks on access control mechanisms, and attacks on client-side handling.
controls.guidance: Implement secure coding practices to prevent common vulnerabilities including injection flaws, XSS, CSRF, broken authentication, and other OWASP Top 10 categories.
evidenceRequirements: Code review records addressing common vulnerabilities

controls.domain: Develop and Maintain Secure Systems and Software
controls.title: Security Vulnerabilities Identified and Managed
controls.requirementText: Security vulnerabilities are identified and managed by installing applicable security patches/updates, and evaluating and following up on applicable security alerts.
controls.guidance: Establish processes to identify security vulnerabilities through vendor alerts, vulnerability databases, and threat intelligence, and address them through patching.

controls.domain: Develop and Maintain Secure Systems and Software
controls.title: Critical Security Patches Installed Timely
controls.requirementText: All system components are protected from known vulnerabilities by installing applicable security patches/updates within one month of release.
controls.guidance: Install critical and high-severity security patches within one month of release for all system components in the cardholder data environment.

controls.domain: Develop and Maintain Secure Systems and Software
controls.title: Public-Facing Web Applications Protected from Attacks
controls.requirementText: For public-facing web applications, new threats and vulnerabilities are addressed on an ongoing basis and these applications are protected against known attacks by reviewing public-facing web applications using automated or manual application vulnerability security assessment tools or methods, at least annually and after any changes; or by installing an automated technical solution that detects and prevents web-based attacks in front of public-facing web applications, to continually check all traffic.
controls.guidance: Protect public-facing web applications through WAF deployment, annual vulnerability assessments, and ongoing threat monitoring.

controls.domain: Develop and Maintain Secure Systems and Software
controls.subdomain: Changes to All System Components Managed Securely
controls.title: Change Control Procedures Established
controls.requirementText: Changes to all system components in the production environment are made according to established procedures that include documentation of impact, documented change approval by authorized parties, functionality testing, and back-out procedures.
controls.guidance: Implement formal change management procedures for all production environment changes including impact assessment, approval, testing, and rollback plans.

controls.domain: Restrict Access to System Components and Cardholder Data by Business Need to Know
controls.subdomain: Access to System Components and Data Appropriately Defined
controls.title: Access Control Model Defined
controls.requirementText: An access control model is defined and includes granting access based on individual personnel's job classification and function, with the least privileges necessary to perform job responsibilities.
controls.guidance: Define and implement an access control model based on role-based access control (RBAC) with least privilege principles.

controls.domain: Restrict Access to System Components and Cardholder Data by Business Need to Know
controls.subdomain: Access to System Components and Data Appropriately Defined
controls.title: Access Assigned Based on Job Classification
controls.requirementText: Access is assigned to users, including privileged users, based on job classification and function, and the principle of least privilege.
controls.guidance: Assign access permissions based on documented job roles and functions, applying the principle of least privilege to all user accounts.

controls.domain: Identify Users and Authenticate Access to System Components
controls.subdomain: User Identification and Related Accounts Managed
controls.title: Unique IDs Assigned to All Users
controls.requirementText: All users are assigned a unique ID before access to system components or cardholder data is allowed.
controls.guidance: Assign unique user IDs to all personnel before granting access to system components or cardholder data to ensure individual accountability.

controls.domain: Identify Users and Authenticate Access to System Components
controls.subdomain: User Identification and Related Accounts Managed
controls.title: Group and Shared Accounts Managed
controls.requirementText: Group, shared, or generic accounts, or other shared authentication credentials are only used when necessary on an exception basis, and are managed with specific additional requirements.
controls.guidance: Minimize and strictly manage the use of group, shared, or generic accounts with documented exceptions and additional controls.

controls.domain: Identify Users and Authenticate Access to System Components
controls.title: All User Access Authenticated
controls.requirementText: All user access to system components for users and administrators is authenticated via at least one of the following authentication factors: something you know, something you have, or something you are.
controls.guidance: Implement authentication mechanisms for all user access using at least one authentication factor (knowledge, possession, or inherence).

controls.domain: Identify Users and Authenticate Access to System Components
controls.title: Minimum Password Complexity Requirements
controls.requirementText: If passwords/passphrases are used as authentication factors, they meet the following minimum level of complexity: a minimum length of 12 characters (or if the system does not support 12 characters, a minimum length of eight characters), containing both numeric and alphabetic characters.
controls.guidance: Enforce minimum password complexity requirements of 12 characters with both numeric and alphabetic characters.

controls.domain: Identify Users and Authenticate Access to System Components
controls.title: MFA for All Access into CDE
controls.requirementText: MFA is implemented for all access into the CDE.
controls.guidance: Implement multi-factor authentication for all access to the cardholder data environment regardless of access method or user type.
evidenceRequirements: MFA implementation records for CDE access
CDE access audit logs showing MFA

controls.domain: Identify Users and Authenticate Access to System Components
controls.title: MFA Systems Properly Configured
controls.requirementText: MFA systems are implemented so that the MFA system is not susceptible to replay attacks, cannot be bypassed by any users including administrative users, and at least two different types of authentication factors are used.
controls.guidance: Configure MFA systems to be resistant to replay attacks, non-bypassable, and requiring two distinct authentication factor types.

controls.domain: Restrict Physical Access to Cardholder Data
controls.title: Security Policies and Procedures Defined
controls.requirementText: All security policies and operational procedures that are identified in Requirement 9 are documented, kept up to date, in use, and known to all affected parties.
controls.guidance: Maintain documented policies and procedures for physical security controls protecting cardholder data.

controls.domain: Restrict Physical Access to Cardholder Data
controls.title: Physical Access Controls in Place
controls.requirementText: Appropriate facility entry controls are in place to limit physical access to systems in the CDE.
controls.guidance: Implement physical access controls (badge readers, locks, mantraps) to restrict access to areas containing cardholder data systems.

controls.domain: Log and Monitor All Access to System Components and Cardholder Data
controls.title: Security Policies and Procedures Defined
controls.requirementText: All security policies and operational procedures that are identified in Requirement 10 are documented, kept up to date, in use, and known to all affected parties.
controls.guidance: Maintain documented policies and procedures for logging and monitoring access to system components and cardholder data.

controls.domain: Log and Monitor All Access to System Components and Cardholder Data
controls.title: Audit Logs Enabled for All System Components
controls.requirementText: Audit logs are enabled and active for all system components and cardholder data.
controls.guidance: Enable audit logging on all system components that store, process, or transmit cardholder data to capture security-relevant events.

controls.domain: Log and Monitor All Access to System Components and Cardholder Data
controls.title: Audit Logs Capture Specific Events
controls.requirementText: Audit logs capture all individual user access to cardholder data, all actions taken by any individual with root or administrative privileges, access to all audit trails, invalid logical access attempts, use of and changes to identification and authentication mechanisms, initialization, stopping, or pausing of audit logs, and creation and deletion of system-level objects.
controls.guidance: Configure audit logs to capture the minimum set of security events required by PCI DSS including user access, admin actions, and system events.

controls.domain: Log and Monitor All Access to System Components and Cardholder Data
controls.title: Audit Logs Read Access Restricted
controls.requirementText: Read access to audit logs files is limited to those with a job-related need.
controls.guidance: Restrict read access to audit log files to only authorized personnel with a documented business need.

controls.domain: Log and Monitor All Access to System Components and Cardholder Data
controls.title: Audit Log Files Protected from Modification
controls.requirementText: Audit log files are protected to prevent modifications by individuals.
controls.guidance: Implement controls to protect audit log files from unauthorized modification including write protection and integrity monitoring.

controls.domain: Log and Monitor All Access to System Components and Cardholder Data
controls.title: Audit Logs Reviewed Daily
controls.requirementText: The following audit logs are reviewed at least once daily: all security events, logs of all system components that store, process, or transmit CHD and/or SAD, logs of all critical system components, logs of all servers and system components that perform security functions.
controls.guidance: Review security event logs daily for all critical systems and components in the cardholder data environment.

controls.domain: Log and Monitor All Access to System Components and Cardholder Data
controls.title: Audit Log History Retained for 12 Months
controls.requirementText: Retain audit log history for at least 12 months, with at least the most recent three months immediately available for analysis.
controls.guidance: Retain all audit logs for a minimum of 12 months with the last three months available for immediate analysis.

controls.domain: Test Security of Systems and Networks Regularly
controls.subdomain: Wireless Access Points Identified and Monitored
controls.title: Authorized and Unauthorized Wireless APs Managed
controls.requirementText: Authorized and unauthorized wireless access points are managed by testing for the presence of wireless (Wi-Fi) access points and detecting and identifying all authorized and unauthorized wireless access points on a quarterly basis.
controls.guidance: Conduct quarterly wireless access point scans to identify and manage both authorized and unauthorized wireless access points.

controls.domain: Test Security of Systems and Networks Regularly
controls.subdomain: External and Internal Vulnerabilities Regularly Identified
controls.title: Internal Vulnerability Scans Performed Quarterly
controls.requirementText: Internal vulnerability scans are performed at least once every three months and after any significant change. High-risk and critical vulnerabilities are resolved. Rescans are performed that confirm all high-risk and critical vulnerabilities have been resolved. The scan tool is kept up to date.
controls.guidance: Perform internal vulnerability scans quarterly and after significant changes, resolving high-risk and critical vulnerabilities with validation rescans.

controls.domain: Test Security of Systems and Networks Regularly
controls.subdomain: External and Internal Vulnerabilities Regularly Identified
controls.title: External Vulnerability Scans Performed Quarterly
controls.requirementText: External vulnerability scans are performed at least once every three months and after any significant change by a PCI SSC Approved Scanning Vendor (ASV). Vulnerabilities are resolved and ASV program guide requirements for a passing scan are met. Rescans are performed as needed.
controls.guidance: Conduct quarterly external vulnerability scans through a PCI SSC-approved scanning vendor (ASV) and remediate identified vulnerabilities.

controls.domain: Test Security of Systems and Networks Regularly
controls.subdomain: External and Internal Penetration Testing Performed
controls.title: Penetration Testing Performed Annually
controls.requirementText: External and internal penetration testing is performed at least once every 12 months and after any significant infrastructure or application upgrade or modification.
controls.guidance: Conduct annual external and internal penetration testing by qualified personnel and after any significant infrastructure or application changes.

controls.domain: Test Security of Systems and Networks Regularly
controls.subdomain: Network Intrusions and File Changes Detected and Responded To
controls.title: Intrusion Detection/Prevention Techniques Deployed
controls.requirementText: Intrusion-detection and/or intrusion-prevention techniques are used to detect and/or prevent intrusions into the network. All traffic at the perimeter of the CDE and at critical points within the CDE is monitored.
controls.guidance: Deploy IDS/IPS solutions to detect and prevent network intrusions at the CDE perimeter and at critical points within the CDE.

controls.domain: Test Security of Systems and Networks Regularly
controls.subdomain: Network Intrusions and File Changes Detected and Responded To
controls.title: Change-Detection Mechanism Deployed
controls.requirementText: A change-detection mechanism (for example, file integrity monitoring tools) is deployed to alert personnel to unauthorized modification of critical system files, configuration files, or content files; and the tools are configured to perform critical file comparisons at least weekly.
controls.guidance: Deploy file integrity monitoring (FIM) on critical system files and configuration files with at least weekly comparisons.

controls.domain: Test Security of Systems and Networks Regularly
controls.subdomain: Unauthorized Changes on Payment Pages Detected and Responded To
controls.title: Payment Page Change Detection
controls.requirementText: A change- and tamper-detection mechanism is deployed on payment pages to alert personnel to unauthorized modification to the HTTP headers and the contents of payment pages as received by the consumer browser.
controls.guidance: Deploy mechanisms to detect unauthorized changes to payment page HTTP headers and content to prevent e-skimming and Magecart-style attacks.

controls.domain: Support Information Security with Organizational Policies and Programs
controls.title: Overall Information Security Policy Established
controls.requirementText: An overall information security policy is established, published, maintained, and disseminated to all relevant personnel, as well as relevant vendors and business partners.
controls.guidance: Establish and maintain a comprehensive information security policy that is communicated to all relevant personnel and third parties.

controls.domain: Support Information Security with Organizational Policies and Programs
controls.subdomain: Risks to the Cardholder Data Environment are Formally Identified
controls.title: Risk Assessment Performed Annually
controls.requirementText: Each PCI DSS requirement that provides flexibility for how frequently it is performed is supported by a targeted risk analysis that is documented and includes justification for the frequency, identification of the assets being protected, identification of threats, and analysis of the likelihood and impact.
controls.guidance: Perform a formal risk assessment annually that identifies threats, vulnerabilities, and their potential impact on the cardholder data environment.

controls.domain: Support Information Security with Organizational Policies and Programs
controls.title: Security Awareness Program Implemented
controls.requirementText: A formal security awareness program is implemented to make all personnel aware of the cardholder data security policy and procedures.
controls.guidance: Implement a formal security awareness program that educates all personnel about the cardholder data security policies and their responsibilities.

controls.domain: Support Information Security with Organizational Policies and Programs
controls.subdomain: Risk to Information Assets from Third-Party Service Provider Relationships is Managed
controls.title: Service Provider Inventory Maintained
controls.requirementText: A list of all third-party service providers with which account data is shared or that could affect the security of account data is maintained, including a description of each of the services provided.
controls.guidance: Maintain an up-to-date inventory of all third-party service providers with access to or that could affect the security of cardholder data.

controls.domain: Support Information Security with Organizational Policies and Programs
controls.subdomain: Suspected and Confirmed Security Incidents that Could Impact the CDE Are Responded to Immediately
controls.title: Incident Response Plan Established
controls.requirementText: An incident response plan exists and is ready to be activated in the event of a suspected or confirmed security incident. The plan addresses roles, responsibilities, and communication and contact strategies including notification of the payment brands, at a minimum; incident response procedures; business recovery and continuity procedures; data backup processes; analysis of legal requirements for reporting compromises; coverage and responses of all critical system components; and reference to or inclusion of incident response procedures from the payment brands.
controls.guidance: Establish and maintain a comprehensive incident response plan covering roles, responsibilities, communication strategies, and payment brand notification procedures.
evidenceRequirements: Annual plan review and testing records

controls.domain: Support Information Security with Organizational Policies and Programs
controls.subdomain: Suspected and Confirmed Security Incidents that Could Impact the CDE Are Responded to Immediately
controls.title: Incident Response Plan Reviewed and Tested Annually
controls.requirementText: The security incident response plan is reviewed and tested at least annually, including all elements listed in Requirement 12.10.1.
controls.guidance: Review and test the incident response plan at least annually through tabletop exercises or simulations to ensure effectiveness.
